name: Asylum Application
description: Asylum application for refugee status determination. Process includes initial registration, personal interview, evidence assessment, and decision making per AsylG requirements.

regulations.title: Anerkennung als Asylberechtigter
regulations.summary: Defines entitlement to asylum under Article 16a of the German Constitution. Persons persecuted for political reasons are entitled to asylum.

regulations.title: Flüchtlingsschutz
regulations.summary: Establishes refugee protection based on Geneva Convention criteria. Persons with well-founded fear of persecution qualify for refugee status.

regulations.title: Subsidiärer Schutz
regulations.summary: Covers subsidiary protection for those facing serious harm in their country of origin including death penalty, torture, or serious threats to life.

regulations.title: Verbot der Abschiebung
regulations.summary: Prohibition of deportation when there is danger to life or freedom in destination country.

regulations.title: Ausschluss vom Flüchtlingsschutz
regulations.summary: Exclusion from refugee protection for serious crimes or acts contrary to UN purposes.

validationRules.condition: Evidence of individual persecution must be credible and specific
validationRules.action: Review documented incidents, verify consistency in testimony, assess credibility of sources

validationRules.condition: Identity must be established through documents or credible testimony
validationRules.action: Verify authenticity of identity documents, cross-check with database, assess credibility

validationRules.condition: Travel route from origin country must be documented
validationRules.action: Verify entry date, document transit countries, check for safe third country exclusion

validationRules.condition: Family members with pending or approved applications must be identified
validationRules.action: Search for related applications, verify family relationships, consider family unity principles

validationRules.condition: Special needs and vulnerabilities must be identified
validationRules.action: Assess for trauma, health conditions, family situation requiring special procedures

commonIssues.suggestion: Identity can be established through alternative means including detailed testimony, language analysis, and country expertise.
Significant inconsistencies in persecution account require clarification. Consider trauma effects on memory.
If applicant traveled through safe third country, application may be inadmissible per Dublin Regulation.
